channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Obama urges Black men to show up for Harris as he campaigns in critical Pennsylvania
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Former President Barack Obama gave a blistering critique of his White House successor Donald Trump and urged Black men to show up for Kamala Harris as he campaigned in Pittsburgh on Thursday at the start of a swing-state tour for the Democratic ticket.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Harris rips Trump for his criticism of federal storm response and calls for 'dignity'
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris and the White House criticized Donald Trump for his attacks on the federal response to Hurricanes Helene and Milton and suggested he was wrongly trying to turn the deadly storms to his political advantage.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT, DARLENE SUPERVILLE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 12 rescued from Colorado gold mine tourist site where elevator mishap killed 1 person
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Twelve people were rescued Thursday night after being trapped for about six hours at the bottom of a former Colorado gold mine when an elevator malfunctioned at the tourist site, authorities said. One person died in the accident.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Utah candidates for Mitt Romney's open US Senate seat debate approaches to climate change
item.description: OGDEN, Utah (AP) — The Republican and Democratic candidates vying for Mitt Romney’s open U.S. Senate seat sought to convince Utah voters on Thursday that they have the better blueprint to tackle climate change and protect the state's vast natural resources. Republican U.S. Rep.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about Hurricane Milton after it slammed Florida's Gulf Coast
item.description: As Hurricane Milton barreled into and across Florida, it spawned dozens of tornadoes, knocked out power to millions, damaged homes, flooded neighborhoods and led to hundreds of rescues. But the damage wasn't as bad as had been feared. Gov.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Tampa sees 5x the usual October rainfall due to Hurricane Milton
item.description: Hurricane Milton's landfall as a Category 3 hurricane didn't just bring devastating storm surge and extremely dangerous wind speeds, it also left Tampa Bay soaked with historic rainfall.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: Pro-Trump group sues fed agencies for allegedly concealing records on assassination attempt suspect Ryan Routh
item.description: A Pro-Trump organization has filed a lawsuit against the DOJ, DHS, DOD, and the State Department for allegedly concealing records on second assassination attempt suspect Ryan Routh.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Milton causes widespread power outages and several deaths in Florida
item.description: Hurricane Milton brought powerful winds, a dangerous storm surge and flooding to much of Florida after making landfall along the Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm. It weakened as it plowed through Florida late Wednesday into Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Massive hole swallows 2 homes in Florida after Milton
item.description: The wild scene raised concerns about whether the ground collapse was caused by a sinkhole or water damage from sewage issues.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, NewsNation

item.title: Anti-Israel agitators deface Harvard statute day after Oct. 7 anniversary: ‘Bring the war home’
item.description: Just after the one­-year anniversary of the Oct. 7 massacre in Israel, demonstrators vandalized Harvard's campus in "an act of solidarity with the Palestinian resistance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, social activist and wife of Robert F. Kennedy, has died
item.description: BOSTON, Mass. (AP) — Ethel Kennedy, the wife of Sen. Robert F. Kennedy, who raised their 11 children after he was assassinated and remained dedicated to social causes and the family’s legacy for decades thereafter, died on Thursday, her family said. She was 96.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rare comet visible in the night sky won't return for 80,000 years
item.description: Comet Tsuchinshan-ATLAS, also known as C/2023 A3 to scientists and pronounced Choo-cheen-shahn, is expected to be visible to the naked eye.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas 3-year-old overdoses on fentanyl after mom travels to Mexico for pills: Police
item.description: “Ashley stated the local pills were too expensive, so she and [another person] decide[d] it was better to return to Mexico and buy the fentanyl from there,” police said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors reviewing 'new evidence' in Marilyn Manson sexual abuse probe
item.description: The sex crimes division of the Los Angeles County District Attorney’s Office is reviewing new leads and evidence in the investigation into shock-rocker Marilyn Manson, who has been accused by multiple women, including “Westworld” actor Evan Rachel Wood, of sexual abuse, officials announced Wednesday
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: How to help the recovery from Milton, Helene
item.description: Nexstar, the parent company of this website, is teaming up to help support Red Cross efforts to offer critical resources after Milton.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KRON4

item.title: Over 135 rescued from Tampa assisted living facility amid 'unprecedented' flooding from Milton
item.description: “This is extraordinary to see this type of flooding, especially in this type of area,” said Hillsborough County Sheriff Chad Chronister.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Atlantic City mayor and his wife plead not guilty to beating their daughter
item.description: MAYS LANDING, N.J. (AP) — The mayor of Atlantic City and his wife, who is the seaside gambling resort's schools superintendent, pleaded not guilty Thursday to beating and abusing their teenage daughter, with a lawyer saying that “parenting struggles are not criminal events.” Mayor Marty Small Sr.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Latest: Hurricane Milton weakens to Category 1, but danger remains in Florida
item.description: Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday along Florida’s Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm, bringing powerful winds, deadly storm surge and potential flooding to much of the state. Milton drew fuel from exceedingly warm Gulf of Mexico waters, twice reaching Category 5 status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lighting and furniture store to pay $4.1M in 'deceptive' pricing lawsuit
item.description: A lighting and furniture company based in California will pay more than $4 million to settle a civil lawsuit that accused the corporation of deceptive pricing and false advertising. Lamps Plus, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Watch: Massive 28-foot wave captured inside Hurricane Milton
item.description: Drone footage captured a monstrous wave inside Hurricane Milton as the powerful storm passed through the Gulf of Mexico on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: 5 dead after a small plane crashes on Santa Catalina Island off the Southern California coast
item.description: AVALON, Calif. (AP) — Five people were killed when a small plane crashed during takeoff from Santa Catalina Island off the Southern California coast, authorities said Wednesday. The twin-engine Beechcraft 95 crashed shortly after 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hurricane Milton slams Florida as a Category 3 storm; leaving millions in the dark
item.description: Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday along Florida’s Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm, bringing powerful winds, deadly storm surge and potential flooding to much of the state. Milton drew fuel from exceedingly warm Gulf of Mexico waters, twice reaching Category 5 status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LOOK: Tornado crosses Florida interstate as Hurricane Milton churns closer
item.description: As Florida's Gulf Coast braces for the impacts of Hurricane Milton Wednesday, the storm is causing severe weather across the state.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, KRON4

item.title: Feeling stressed about the election? Here's what some are doing and what they say you can do too
item.description: If the high stakes presidential election is causing troublesome thoughts, existential dread or rifts with loved ones, there’s no need to white knuckle through it. Take a deep breath. Literally.
item.dc:creator: MARIAM FAM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Plane makes emergency landing in New York City after pilot dies mid-flight
item.description: Crew members on the Turkish Airlines flight worked to revive the pilot, but he died before the plane landed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton slams Florida as a Category 3 storm; leaving millions in the dark
item.description: Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday along Florida’s Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm, bringing powerful winds, deadly storm surge and potential flooding to much of the state. Milton drew fuel from exceedingly warm Gulf of Mexico waters, twice reaching Category 5 status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, widow of Robert F. Kennedy, hospitalized after suffering stroke, family says
item.description: Ethel Kennedy, 96, is former President John F. Kennedy's sister-in-law and mother to 11 children, including former independent presidential candidate Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, WGN 9

item.title: Biden postpones trip to Germany and Angola to monitor Hurricane Milton
item.description: President Joe Biden said Tuesday he is postponing a planned trip to Germany and Angola to stay at the White House to monitor Hurricane Milton, as it spins closer to Florida’s Gulf Coast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Members of LDS Church, Utahns help those affected by Helene, prepare for Milton
item.description: Members of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints along with a Davis County business and the American Red Cross are helping many pick up the pieces after Hurricane Helene ravaged the southeast.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, ABC 4

item.title: Why do people fill their bathtubs with water before a hurricane?
item.description: If you live in a hurricane-prone area, it may be common practice for you. If you live well outside that region, you may be confused by the move.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Mama bear defeats rival that killed her cub to win Fat Bear Week 2024
item.description: The brown bears truly ate this year, and we mean they ate a lot of salmon at Katmai National Park and Preserve.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Parsons, ABC 4

item.title: Florida has nearly all ballots counted on Election Day, while California can take weeks. This is why
item.description: Why is it that some states have nearly all the ballots counted within hours of polls closing while others take weeks?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Beloved father fighting for life in ICU after house explosion in Long Beach
item.description: The man injured in a violent house explosion on Monday is fighting for his life in the intensive care unit, his family tells KTLA.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, Fox 5

item.title: Plane evacuating people from Hurricane Milton crashes after takeoff
item.description: A plane carrying passengers evacuating from Hurricane Milton crashed near the southeast corner of the St. Pete Pier Tuesday morning, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9

item.title: What makes a storm a hurricane? The dangers across 5 categories
item.description: Severe weather can target any part of the globe, but each year the Atlantic hurricane season brings heavy rain, strong wind, storm surge and sometimes catastrophic devastation to locations from the Caribbean islands and Central America all the way up through the United States and into Canada.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Airline 'reviewing' incident where explicit movie was played on every screen
item.description: Australia's Qantas Airways is apologizing after showing a movie that contains scenes of nudity, graphic language and sexting to the passengers aboard a recent flight from Sydney to Haneda, Japan.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KTLA 5

item.title: Why is Tampa Bay so vulnerable to hurricanes?
item.description: As Milton barrels toward the Gulf Coast of Florida, experts are worried that a century of luck could come to a sudden end.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
